cross_scene_arcs.title: The Call of Freedom vs. Societal Duty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: From childhood fascination with the untamed desert to her final moments en route to an arranged marriage, Jen's internal struggle between personal freedom and societal obligation persists. Early signs of rebellion grow into active resistance, yet she remains trapped by external expectations, culminating in a forced wedding journey where her silence speaks volumes.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Young Jen feels disconnected from her mother's world, drawn instead to the wild beauty of the desert, signaling the birth of her yearning for autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen is physically confined in a wedding carriage, moving toward a life she did not choose, her agency stripped away despite her inner resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Jen's ultimate choice between duty and desire remains suspended, with no resolution in sight as she is carried forward by forces beyond her control.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Warrior Fantasy to Martial Reality
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jen's romanticized vision of warrior freedom evolves into a violent, isolating reality as she asserts her martial identity through defiance and combat. Her journey from handling the Green Destiny with awe to dueling Li Mu Bai atop bamboo forests marks a transformation from aspirant to outlaw, driven by a need to prove herself outside any master's shadow.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jen idealizes the warrior life, seeing the Green Destiny sword as a symbol of total freedom and adventure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen is disarmed and nearly killed by Li Mu Bai, her defiance broken but not her spirit, forcing her to confront the cost of her rebellion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Jen's belief in absolute freedom through strength is challenged by her defeat and dependence on Jade Fox, leaving her martial identity in crisis.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Bond with Lo: Love, Loss, and Reunion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jen's relationship with Lo evolves from reluctant dependency to passionate reunion, only to end in transcendent separation. Their bond persists through silence, symbols, and sacrifice, representing a love that is deeply felt but ultimately unsustainable in the mortal world.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Injured and isolated, Jen reluctantly accepts help from Lo, their relationship strained by distrust and past betrayal.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen and Lo share a final, sacred union before she leaps into the clouds, choosing spiritual freedom over earthly love.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether their love was truly fulfilled or tragically incomplete remains ambiguous, as Jen vanishes into myth, leaving Lo behind.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rejection and Redemption: The Path from Rebellion to Atonement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jen's arc shifts from defiant rejection of all authority—Li Mu Bai, Yu, and societal norms—to a quiet path of responsibility and redemption. After betraying her mentors and embracing outlaw life, she ultimately chooses to save Li Mu Bai, reclaiming her moral agency and earning forgiveness.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jen rejects Li Mu Bai's offer of discipleship, choosing rebellion over discipline and mentorship.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen is absolved by Yu and given a new purpose—journeying to Wudan Mountain to seek truth and self-mastery, marking her transformation from fugitive to seeker.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The nature of Jen's future path remains uncertain; she must now define herself not through defiance, but through self-chosen discipline.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Symbolic Tokens and Shifting Allegiances
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Objects like the jade comb and Yu's hairpin serve as markers of Jen's changing status—from betrothed woman to fugitive, then to redeemed agent of salvation. These tokens reflect her shifting relationships and social standing, moving from symbols of lost love to instruments of trust and legitimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The return of the jade comb reopens Jen's emotional wounds, symbolizing the unresolved tension between love and duty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen uses Yu's hairpin and her own actions to gain entry and trust at the Yuan compound, now seen not as a thief but as a bearer of salvation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While Jen regains symbolic legitimacy, her ultimate place in any community remains undefined, as she prepares to leave for Wudan Mountain alone.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Captor to Lover: The Evolution of Lo and Jen's Relationship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Lo's relationship with Jen evolves from violent abduction and dominance to deep emotional intimacy and spiritual union, tracing a transformation from captor to vulnerable lover. Their bond shifts through stages of conflict, care, revelation, passion, separation, and transcendence, ultimately culminating in a love that survives physical absence through mythic resonance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Lo asserts physical control over Jen as a captured prize.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Lo accepts a spiritual, transcendent connection with Jen after her final departure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether their love can exist beyond myth and longing, or if it is fated to remain unfulfilled in the material world.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Quest for Belonging: Lo's Identity Beyond the Bandit
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Lo's personal journey shifts from a life defined by outlawry and survival to a deeper pursuit of emotional legitimacy, belonging, and purpose. He moves from seeking power and control to desiring acceptance—by Jen, her family, and society—culminating in a transformation from solitary bandit to a man capable of love, sacrifice, and spiritual peace.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Lo identifies as a hardened outlaw, masking inner loneliness with toughness.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Lo embraces emotional vulnerability and finds peace in a transcendent bond, no longer defined by material control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Lo's societal reintegration remains incomplete; his identity is resolved emotionally but not socially.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Comb as Covenant: Symbolic Exchange and Broken Promises
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The jade comb serves as a central symbol of possession, trust, and emotional commitment between Lo and Jen. It passes from Jen to Lo as a token of love, is returned as a sign of rejection, reappears as a pledge, and is finally left behind—marking the arc of their relationship through its physical presence and absence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Lo takes the comb as a symbol of conquest.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jen leaves the comb behind, transforming it into a relic of transcendent love.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The comb's final meaning—abandonment or eternal bond—remains ambiguous, open to interpretation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Alliance: Lo's Shifting Position in the Martial World
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Lo transitions from a solitary, enigmatic figure operating on the margins to someone embedded in the network of the martial world. Initially observed indirectly, he becomes a protected ally under Li Mu Bai and Yu's influence, shifting from fugitive to legitimate actor with recognized emotional stakes.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Lo's influence is implied but unseen; he exists on the periphery of key events.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Lo is positioned as a sanctioned romantic prospect, exiled but protected, integrated into the moral order.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Lo's past affiliations (e.g., with Jade Fox) and full trustworthiness remain unverified, leaving his status precarious.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cycle of Violence and Care: Power, Betrayal, and Redemption
The Resurgence of Forbidden Love
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Yu's repressed emotional connection with Li Mu Bai resurfaces upon his return, evolving from silent tension to reluctant acknowledgment and finally to a moment of intimate vulnerability, revealing the enduring power of their bond despite societal and spiritual constraints.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Yu is emotionally guarded, maintaining composure as Li Mu Bai reenters her life.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Yu and Li openly express their love, though still burdened by guilt and duty.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Their love remains in conflict with the ascetic ideals of the Jiang Hu, leaving their future together uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion and Reclamation of Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Yu's authority as a figure of power is challenged by the theft of her sword, triggering a crisis that forces her to confront vulnerabilities in her household and rethink her reliance on formal power, shifting toward strategic, behind-the-scenes control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Yu seeks to understand how to wield authority in Peking through institutional means.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Yu begins embracing informal networks and indirect influence, recognizing the limitations of official status.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Yu has not yet reconciled her official role with the morally ambiguous tactics required to maintain real power.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mother and Daughter: Duty vs. Freedom
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Yu's strained relationship with Jen intensifies as their opposing values—duty and tradition versus freedom and self-discovery—culminate in a complete breakdown of trust and familial bond, symbolizing a generational clash within the Jiang Hu.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Yu attempts to guide Jen with wisdom and restraint, seeing herself in the girl's inner conflict.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Yu disowns Jen after a final confrontation, severing their relationship in defense of duty and honor.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Jen will return or continue her rebellion, and if reconciliation is possible, remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Sword as Burden and Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The Green Destiny Sword evolves from a symbol of Li Mu Bai's emotional burden to a catalyst for conspiracy, investigation, and ultimately a lifeline—passing through Yu's hands as both a duty and a link to multiple destinies.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Yu accepts the sword as a personal and emotional charge from Li Mu Bai.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Yu entrusts the sword's legacy to Jen by sending her to save Li Mu Bai, completing a cycle of transmission and sacrifice.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The sword's ultimate fate and its role in shaping Jen's path remain open-ended.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Strategist
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Yu transforms from a reactive figure responding to events into an active agent shaping the course of the mission, taking initiative in investigating Jade Fox, redirecting strategies, protecting key figures, and ultimately driving the effort to save Li Mu Bai.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Yu begins investigating the 'Jade Fox' clue independently, showing personal investment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Yu assumes full strategic and emotional responsibility, orchestrating the antidote mission through Jen.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Yu's deeper motives—especially regarding Lo and her defiance of Li Mu Bai—remain partially concealed, suggesting hidden layers to her agenda.
